standard_notation: Z \to b\bar{s}+\bar{b}s,\ Z \to b\bar{d}+\bar{b}d,\ Z \to s\bar{d}+\bar{s}d
implementation_difficulty_reason: External-limit bookkeeping is low effort, but a production RS constraint needs new electroweak Delta F=1 matching for off-diagonal Z down-quark couplings, custodial-embedding conventions, and a policy for combining direct Z-width and low-energy flavor-derived coupling bounds.
priority_rationale: Deferred by DA-4 (round_004_addendum_deferred_scope.md); Weak FCNC-Z tails; no dedicated experimental search at LEP era; LHC limits weak. Promoted in Wave-8 because: Top-tier per SESSION_NOTES.md sec 7; the only standalone FCNC-Z analysis row in the catalog (distinct from drafted Z-pole T010-T013 and LFV Z T015-T017). Lower implementation priority than Waves 1-7 PRIMARY entries.

evidence: Targeted grep for Z -> bs/bd/sd, Zbs/Zbd/Zsd, FCNC-Z, and gL/gR down-sector Z-coupling strings returned no hits.
The implemented modern phenomenology surface is Delta F=2 neutral-meson mixing only: quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23.
Generic Z references are alpha_s/M_Z support, not FCNC-Z observables: qcd/constants.py:11 and quarkConstraints/qcd_running.py:100.
Only unrelated charged-lepton dipole LFV appears in flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75.
grep_commands: rg -n "Z.?->.?b.?s|Z.?->.?s.?b|Z.?->.?b.?d|Z.?->.?d.?b|Z.?->.?s.?d|Z.?->.?d.?s|Zbs|Zbd|Zsd|bsZ|bdZ|sdZ|flavou?r.?violat.*Z|FCNC.*Z|Z.*FCNC|gLbs|gRbs|gLbd|gRbd|gLsd|gRsd" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "b.?->.?s|b.?->.?d|s.?->.?d|Delta B|Delta S|K\\*|B_s.*mu|B_d.*mu|K.*nu|pi.*nu|Z.?penguin|penguin|dipole|epsilon.?K|Delta m_K|Delta m_B|B_s|B_d|D0|mu.?e.?gamma|Z.?->|h.?->" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_delta_mk|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma|Wilson|operator|likelihood" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"
rg -n "R_b|A_FB|Zbb|Z b|M_Z|mZ|Gamma_Z|GammaZ|Z pole|Z-pole|weak mixing|sin2" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

supporting_measurements.role: Inclusive PDG hadronic-Z width context used by later sources to derive the generic non-standard hadronic-Z bound; not a T014 FCNC observable.

auxiliary_theory_inputs.role: Updated SM branching-ratio expectations; theory inputs, not measured observables.
Future-collider reach projections; not current experimental limits.

values.observable: FCC-ee projected B(Z -> bs) reach with 0.1% systematics
FCC-ee projected B(Z -> bd) reach using only b-tagging

post_2010_developments.why_it_matters: Directly analyzes tree-level flavor-changing Z exchange in custodial warped models for rare K and B decays.
Updates SM Z -> bs/bd expectations, current direct bound, indirect constraints, and FCC-ee reach.
Consolidates FV-Z direct bounds and future H/EW/top-factory projections.
Dedicated quark-sector FV-Z coupling-bound survey including bs, bd, and sd.

status_history.reason: Initialized Wave-8 SECONDARY T014 draft under prompted policy.
Initial PKA draft, source snapshots, code-coverage grep, and sidecar metadata.
WA-w8-T014 cycle-1 polish for Wave-8 SECONDARY FCNC-Z batch.
Opus round 4 per-process sign-off; CA-verified PASS retained; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_004_index.md.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(Z\to b\bar s+\bar b s\),
\(Z\to b\bar d+\bar b d\), and \(Z\to s\bar d+\bar s d\).
This entry covers on-shell flavor-changing neutral-current \(Z\)-boson decays
to unequal down-type quark flavors.
sections.pdg_prose:
The PDG 2025 \(Z\)-boson listing does not provide a dedicated row for
\(Z\to bs\), \(Z\to bd\), or \(Z\to sd\).  The current experiment-equivalent
direct bound used by recent FCNC-\(Z\) summaries is instead the inclusive
non-standard hadronic-width constraint,
\[
  {\cal B}(Z\to q_i\bar q_j+\bar q_i q_j) < 2.9\times10^{-3}
  \qquad (95\%~{\rm CL},\ i\ne j),
\]
from the agreement of the measured \(Z\) hadronic width with the SM
prediction.  For this entry the sidecar records the same direct bound for
\(bs\), \(bd\), and \(sd\), with the \(bs\) and \(bd\) provenance anchored in
the ECFA 2025 and Kamenik et al. 2024 extracts and the explicit \(sd\)
application anchored in Abu-Ajamieh et al. 2026.  The supporting PDG snapshot
records the inclusive hadronic \(Z\) branching fraction used as context for
the width comparison, but that inclusive quantity is not itself a T014 FCNC
observable.
sections.relevance:
Anarchic warped models naturally generate flavor-nonuniversal neutral-gauge
couplings after rotating the localized fermion zero modes to the mass basis.
For down-type quarks this creates off-diagonal \(Z d_i d_j\) vertices through
zero-mode coupling nonuniversality, mixing with KK gauge bosons, and mixing
with KK fermions.  The custodial construction that protects \(Zb_L\bar b_L\)
also matters for off-diagonal left-handed \(Z d_L^i\bar d_L^j\) couplings;
in custodial RS, rare \(K\) and \(B\) amplitudes can instead be dominated by
right-handed tree-level \(Z\) exchange.  The direct on-shell \(Z\to q_iq_j\)
bound is therefore a weak but conceptually clean collider diagnostic for the
same effective vertices that enter \(K\), \(B_d\), and \(B_s\) rare decays and
mixing.  Per \texttt{flavor\_catalog/PRIORITY\_TIERS.md}, T014 is a Wave-8
\textbf{SECONDARY} entry: it provides the catalog's standalone FCNC-\(Z\)
row, but has lower implementation priority than the Wave 1--7 PRIMARY
constraints.
sections.post_2008:
Blanke, Buras, Duling, Gemmler, and Gori 2009 is the key RS-specific update
relative to the CFW era: it studies rare \(K\) and \(B\) decays in a custodial
warped model and shows how tree-level \(Z\) exchange with flavor-changing
down-quark couplings can dominate selected \(\Delta F=1\) amplitudes.  Kamenik
et al. 2024 updated the SM predictions, current direct-width bound, and
future-collider reach for the \(bs\) and \(bd\) modes; those theory and
projection numbers are retained in the sidecar rather than treated as T014
measured observables.  The ECFA 2025 report folds this program into the
Higgs/electroweak/top factory studies.  Abu-Ajamieh et al. 2026 gives a
dedicated quark-sector survey of flavor-violating \(Z\) couplings, translating
the common direct width bound into effective-coupling conventions and comparing
it with much stronger meson-oscillation constraints.
sections.validity:
The direct \(2.9\times10^{-3}\) limit is flavor-inclusive and not a dedicated
\(bs\), \(bd\), or \(sd\) analysis; it should not be treated as a powerful
standalone RS exclusion.  Low-energy constraints from \(K\), \(B_d\), and
\(B_s\) mixing or rare decays are much stronger, but they test model-dependent
linear combinations of left- and right-handed \(Z\) couplings and can be
affected by correlations or cancellations with KK-gauge and other
\(\Delta F=1,2\) contributions.  A live implementation therefore needs an
explicit convention for off-diagonal \(Z d_i d_j\) couplings and a decision on
whether to impose only the weak direct width bound or the stronger
low-energy-derived coupling bounds.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  A targeted grep for \(Z\to bs\), \(Z\to bd\), \(Z\to sd\),
\texttt{Zbs}/\texttt{Zbd}/\texttt{Zsd}, and flavor-violating \(Z\)-coupling
strings across \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and
\texttt{tests/} returned no hits.  Broader searches find only existing
\(\Delta F=2\) neutral-meson machinery
(\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}) and generic
\(M_Z\)/\(\alpha_s(M_Z)\) support such as
\texttt{qcd/constants.py:11} and \texttt{quarkConstraints/qcd\_running.py:100}.
There is no on-shell FCNC-\(Z\) branching-ratio likelihood or \(Z d_i d_j\)
operator interface.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  Recording the external direct limit is straightforward, but a
production RS constraint requires new electroweak \(\Delta F=1\) matching for
off-diagonal \(Z\) couplings, custodial-embedding bookkeeping, and a policy for
correlating direct \(Z\)-width information with already-implemented
\(\Delta F=2\) and future rare-decay constraints.  The current code only
covers neutral-meson mixing and unrelated charged-lepton dipoles.

priority_rationale_short: Direct down-FCNC Z width bound is low-energy dominated
priority_rationale_full: The direct Z -> bs, bd, sd branching-fraction limit is conceptually clean but very weak as a standalone collider bound. The sidecar's auxiliary theory inputs correctly note that K, B_d, and B_s mixing or rare decays imply much stronger effective Z d_i d_j coupling constraints. Because this entry is dominated by the existing low-energy flavor program and is a SECONDARY tail, it ranks LOW for direct scan power.
